title: The Opening
story_beat: Pink Bear wakes up to a snowy Easter morning, setting the scene and hinting at the unexpected challenge ahead for her and Susie.

techniques.name: character_voice
techniques.reason: Chris has not yet been assessed on character voice, and establishing Pink Bear's unique way of thinking and feeling is crucial for the story's opening.

techniques.name: sensory_detail
techniques.reason: Chris is competent in sensory detail, so a brief reminder will help bring Pink Bear's snowy Easter morning to life with vivid sights, sounds, and feelings.

techniques.name: show_dont_tell
techniques.reason: Chris is competent in showing rather than telling; a quick refresh will ensure Pink Bear's emotions and the snowy setting are conveyed through actions and descriptions, not just statements.

wonder_prompt: Imagine your best friend is telling you a story. How do you know it's *them*? What makes their voice unique, even if you can't see them? Today, we'll explore how to give Pink Bear a voice so distinct, readers will feel like they know her heart.
wonder_example: I must go, said the bear. I am a bear of very little brain, and I have lost my way. He sat down on a log and began to think. He thought and he thought and he thought. (A.A. Milne, Winnie-the-Pooh)
build_instructions: As Pink Bear wakes up to a snowy Easter, focus on introducing her unique voice and the world of Susie's bedroom and garden, subtly hinting at the unexpected challenge of rebuilding Easter.
page_prompts: Write the moment Pink Bear first wakes up on Easter morning. What does she see, hear, or feel that tells her something is different outside Susie's window?
Pink Bear realizes the snow means no outdoor egg hunt with Susie. Write the exact moment her deep disappointment settles in, showing her feelings through actions or inner thoughts.
As Pink Bear watches Susie sleep, a tiny spark of an idea or a determined thought begins to form in her mind about how to make Easter special. Write that first hint of her resolve.
reflect_preview: Pink Bear has a tiny idea, but how can one small bear bring Easter magic back when the whole world is covered in snow? Next time, we'll see her first brave steps!
